section 141; and
the amendments made to the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table by the amendment Act.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 43: inserted, on 25 May 2022, by section 5(a) of the Customs and Excise (Tobacco Products) Amendment Act 2022 (2022 No 28).

part.heading: Provisions relating to 2019 and 2020 increases in rates of excise duty for Excise item 99.44

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 5: inserted, on 30 November 2022, by section 30(a) of the Statutes Amendment Act 2022 (2022 No 75).

prov.heading: Adjustment of rates of excise duty for Excise item 99.44

para.text: The Governor-General may, by Order in Council, amend the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table to change the rates of duty that apply to Excise item 99.44.

The new rates must not exceed what the rates would have been on the date on which the order is made had the rates that applied on 30 June 2019 been increased by the maximum amount permitted under clause 21 of Schedule 3 on 1 July 2019 and each subsequent 1 July until the order under this clause is made.
Only 1 order may be made under this clause, and it cannot be made more than 12 months after this clause comes into force.
An order made under this clause is taken to be an order made under clause 21 of Schedule 3, and this Act (other than clause 21(4) of Schedule 3) applies accordingly.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 44: inserted, on 30 November 2022, by section 30(a) of the Statutes Amendment Act 2022 (2022 No 75).

part.heading: Provisions relating to Customs and Excise (Arrival Information) Amendment Act 2023

history.history-note: Schedule 1 Part 6: inserted, on 21 June 2023, by section 7 of the Customs and Excise (Arrival Information) Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 21).

prov.heading: Application of section 28A to arrivals on Defence Force ships and cruise ships

para.text: Section 28A applies to a person arriving in New Zealand on one of the following kinds of ship only on or after 31 October 2023:
a ship under the control of the Defence Force:
a ship operated as part of an international cruise business.

history.history-note: Schedule 1 clause 45: inserted, on 21 June 2023, by section 7 of the Customs and Excise (Arrival Information) Amendment Act 2023 (2023 No 21).

schedule.heading: Application of Act to Defence Force

prov.heading: Requirements relating to arrival and departure of craft and persons

para.text: Subpart 1 of Part 3 does not apply to any member of the Armed Forces, or any craft under the control of the Defence Force, during any period when that member or craft is involved in dealing with an emergency.
Subclause (3) applies if a craft under the control of the Defence Force arrives in, or departs from, New Zealand while involved in dealing with an emergency.
The Chief of Defence Force must, within 48 hours of the craft’s arrival or departure or any longer period that the chief executive allows,—
notify Customs of the craft’s arrival or departure; and
provide Customs with any details relating to goods or persons on the craft that are required by the chief executive.
In this clause, emergency means—

an emergency due to an actual or imminent attack on New Zealand by an enemy, or to any actual or imminent warlike act whether directed against New Zealand or not, if loss of life or injury or distress to persons or danger to the safety of the public is caused or threatened to be caused in New Zealand or in any part of New Zealand; or
a search and rescue event at any point outside New Zealand involving a serious and imminent threat to the safety of persons or craft; or
a state of war or other like emergency in any place outside New Zealand; or
any other circumstances agreed between the chief executive and the Chief of Defence Force.

prov.heading: Customs powers

para.text: The powers in Part 4 may be exercised in relation to the following only in prescribed circumstances:
a member of the Armed Forces:
access to a defence area:
a craft under the control of the Defence Force.

subpart.heading: Excise duty levied
subpart.crosshead: Excise duty levied in respect of Part A goods

prov.heading: Excise duty levied in respect of Part A goods manufactured in New Zealand

para.text: Excise duty is levied in respect of any Part A goods that are manufactured in a manufacturing area.
Subclause (1) is subject to clause 6.
Excise duty is levied in respect of any Part A goods that are manufactured at a place that is not a manufacturing area in contravention of section 56(1)﻿(a).

prov.heading: Excise duty is debt owed to the Crown

para.text: Excise duty levied in respect of any Part A goods is a debt—
due to the Crown immediately when—
the goods are removed for home consumption, if the excise duty is levied under clause 1(1); or
the goods are manufactured, if the excise duty is levied under clause 1(3); and
owed by the persons referred to in subclause (2); and
recoverable by the chief executive (on behalf of the Crown) in any court of competent jurisdiction.
The following persons owe the excise duty levied in respect of any Part A goods:
the occupier of the place where the goods are manufactured:
every person who is the owner of the goods when they are manufactured:
every person who becomes the owner of the goods after they are manufactured but before the excise duty is fully paid.
To avoid doubt, the right to recover excise duty as a debt due to the Crown is not affected by the fact that—
the Part A goods have ceased to be subject to the control of Customs; or
a bond or any other security has been given for the payment of duty; or
no proper assessment of duty has been made under this Act; or
a deficient assessment of duty has been made under this Act.
If excise duty is owed by more than 1 person, the debt is owed by all of them jointly and severally.

prov.heading: Removal for home consumption

para.text: This clause applies, for the purposes of this Act, to Part A goods that are manufactured in a manufacturing area (subject to subclause (6)).
Part A goods are removed for home consumption if the goods are physically removed from a Customs-controlled area, except where—
the goods are moved to another Customs-controlled area under a permit or other authorisation granted by the chief executive under section 85(1)﻿(b); or
the goods are temporarily removed under section 235(1); or
the goods are removed—
for export; or
to an export warehouse.
Part A goods that are treated as being manufactured in a manufacturing area by clause 8(2) or 9(2) are removed for home consumption when they are manufactured.

Part A goods that are used, or that are destroyed, lost, or otherwise physically disposed of, without having been removed for home consumption under subclause (2) or (3) are removed for home consumption when they are used or are destroyed, lost, or otherwise physically disposed of.
If Part A goods are used, without having been removed for home consumption under subclause (2) or (3), in a manufacturing area in the manufacture of other Part A goods,—
subclause (4) does not apply to the goods that are used; and
the excise duty levied under clause 1(1) in respect of those goods does not become a debt due to the Crown.
If Part A goods are exported and depart from New Zealand without having been removed for home consumption,—
this clause does not apply to the goods in respect of any time after they have departed from New Zealand; and
the excise duty levied under clause 1(1) in respect of the goods does not become a debt due to the Crown; but
Part 2 of this schedule (excise-equivalent duty) may apply to the goods if they are imported back.

prov.heading: Rate of excise duty

para.text: Excise duty levied in respect of any goods is levied at the applicable rate of duty specified in Part A of the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table.
If the rate is, or includes, an ad valorem rate, the value of the goods must be determined in accordance with subpart 2 of this Part for the purpose of applying the ad valorem rate.

prov.heading: Time for payment of excise duty

para.text: Except as otherwise provided in this Act,—
excise duty levied under clause 1(1) must be paid to Customs within the prescribed time:
excise duty levied under clause 1(3) must be paid to Customs when the Part A goods are manufactured.

prov.heading: Excise duty not levied on alcohol manufactured in manufacturing area for personal use

para.text: Clause 1(1) does not apply to beer manufactured in a manufacturing area in quantities of not more than 100 litres per month by an individual who is not the licensee of the manufacturing area if—
the beer is manufactured exclusively for the individual’s personal use and not for sale or other disposition to any other person; and
the licensee does not participate in any aspect or part of the brewing or bottling operation and provides no equipment, ingredients, or service other than the following:
the equipment for the brewing and bottling of beer and the filling of kegs:
kegs, bottles, bottle caps, and labels:
the ingredients for brewing beer, in an unmixed and unfermented state:
the written or oral instructions for the manufacture of the beer.
Clause 1(1) does not apply to wine manufactured in a manufacturing area in quantities of not more than 45 litres per month by an individual who is not the licensee of the manufacturing area if—
the wine is manufactured exclusively for the individual’s personal use and not for sale or other disposition to any other person; and
the licensee does not participate in any aspect or part of the wine making or bottling operation and provides no equipment, ingredients, or service other than the following:
the equipment for the making and bottling of wine and the filling of casks or vats:
casks, vats, bottles, corks, and labels:
the ingredients for making wine, in an unmixed and unfermented state:
the written or oral instructions for the manufacture of the wine.
In subclause (2), wine means any goods specified in any of items 99.20.20L, 99.25.20B, 99.30.21D, 99.30.26E, 99.30.32K, and 99.30.47H in Part A of the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table.

prov.heading: Rectification or compounding of spirits

para.text: This clause applies if—
a quantity of spirits is supplied to a place to be rectified or compounded; and
after making an allowance of not more than 2% on that quantity, the volume of alcohol rectified or compounded at that place is less than the volume supplied.
The excise duty levied in respect of the spirits as rectified or compounded includes excise duty on the deficiency referred to in subclause (1)﻿(b).
For the purposes of this clause,—

compounding means the manufacture of spirits (other than perfume, culinary essences, or medicinal or toilet preparations) by imparting a flavour to, or mixing a material or ingredient with, spirits by a method of which the process of distillation is part
rectifying, in relation to spirits, means purifying by a process of redistillation.

prov.heading: Compression of natural gas in compressed natural gas fuelling facility

para.text: This clause applies if the licensee of a manufacturing area supplies natural gas to a compressed natural gas fuelling facility that is not a manufacturing area.
For the purposes of this Act,—
any compression of the natural gas for use as a motor vehicle fuel that is done in the facility is treated as being done by the licensee in the licensee’s manufacturing area; and
any resulting compressed natural gas is treated as being manufactured by the licensee in that manufacturing area accordingly.

prov.heading: Blending of biofuel in biofuel fuelling facility

para.text: This clause applies if the licensee of a manufacturing area supplies biofuel to a biofuel fuelling facility.
For the purposes of this Act,—
any blending of the biofuel that is done in the facility is treated as being done by the licensee in the licensee’s manufacturing area; and
any resulting biofuel blend is treated as being manufactured by the licensee in that manufacturing area accordingly.
Subclause (2) does not apply to any blending of biofuel that is done under section 67(4)﻿(c).
In this clause, biofuel fuelling facility—
means any installation, facility, or other place that—
is used for fuelling any craft, vehicle, or other conveyance; and
is not a Customs-controlled area or a place that is exempted under section 62; and
includes any vehicle designed for the storage and transport of fuel in which a process of blending occurs.

prov.heading: Other special rules relating to removal of motor spirits from Customs-controlled areas and blending

para.text: Regulations may provide that the chief executive must not exercise the chief executive’s power under section 85(1)﻿(b) or 235(1) in respect of dutiable motor spirits.
Regulations made for the purposes of subclause (1) may—
relate to a specified Customs-controlled area or a specified class of Customs-controlled areas:
apply in all circumstances or in prescribed circumstances only.
Subclauses (4) and (5) apply if—
dutiable motor spirits (the new dutiable motor spirits) are manufactured in a manufacturing area by the blending of—
dutiable motor spirits, other than waste motor spirits; with
a minor and incidental quantity of other substances, which may include waste motor spirits; and
before the manufacture of the new dutiable motor spirits began, the dutiable motor spirits covered by paragraph (a)﻿(i) (the original dutiable motor spirits) were—
removed for home consumption; or
entered in accordance with section 75 and the entry passed for home consumption; and
the prescribed conditions (if any) are met.

The excise duty levied under clause 1(1) in respect of the new dutiable motor spirits is levied only in respect of the volume of the new dutiable motor spirits that is attributable to the substances covered by subclause (3)﻿(a)﻿(ii) (the other substances).
Credit may be claimed under section 107 for any excise duty or excise-equivalent duty levied in respect of the original dutiable motor spirits only if, and to the extent, permitted by regulations.
Regulations may, for the purposes of subclause (4), prescribe how the volume of the new dutiable motor spirits that is attributable to the other substances is to be determined.
Regulations may, in consequence of regulations made for the purposes of subclause (4) in accordance with subclause (6), provide that credit—
may not be claimed under section 107 for excise duty or excise-equivalent duty levied in respect of any of the other substances; or
may be so claimed only to the extent permitted by the regulations.
In subclause (4), the reference to the excise duty levied under clause 1(1) includes, if relevant, the following:
any levy imposed by section 213(2)﻿(c) of the Accident Compensation Act 2001:
any levy imposed by section 24 of the Energy (Fuels, Levies, and References) Act 1989.
In this clause,—
dutiable motor spirits means motor spirits—
that are Part A goods and that are manufactured in a manufacturing area; or
that are goods specified in Part B of the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table and that are imported
motor spirits means any fuels that are—
motor spirit, or fuels that contain motor spirit; or
specified in any of the following items:
items 99.75.05F, 99.75.13G, 99.75.23D, 99.75.29C, 99.75.33A, 99.75.37D, 99.75.51K, 99.75.59E, 99.75.73L, 99.75.81A, and 99.75.93E in Part A of the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table:

prov.heading: Value of goods for excise duty if goods sold by licensee

para.text: The value of any goods that are sold by the licensee of the manufacturing area in which they are manufactured is the price at which the goods are sold exclusive of excise duty and goods and services tax if—
the sale is a sale in the open market as defined in clause 13(1)﻿(c); and
the sale is made on or before the date on which the goods are removed from the manufacturing area.
This clause does not apply to goods that a contractor is treated as having manufactured under section 5(3).

prov.heading: Default rule if value of goods cannot be determined under clause 11

para.text: If the value of any goods cannot be determined under clause 11, the value of the goods is their fair market value, as determined under clause 13, at the date on which the event referred to in clause 2(1)﻿(a)﻿(i) or (ii) (as the case requires) occurs.

prov.heading: Fair market value

para.text: For the purposes of this clause,—

identical goods means goods that are the same in all respects, including physical characteristics, quality, and reputation, as the goods being valued, except for minor differences (if any) in appearance that do not affect the value of the goods:

similar goods means goods that closely resemble the goods being valued in respect of component materials and parts and characteristics and that are functionally and commercially interchangeable with the goods being valued, having regard to the quality and reputation of the goods being valued:
a sale in the open market means—
that the price is the sole consideration; and

that the price is not influenced by any commercial, financial, or other ties, whether by contract or otherwise, between the seller, or any person associated in business with the seller, and the buyer, or any person associated in business with the buyer (other than the relationship created by the sale of the goods in question); and

that no part of the proceeds of any subsequent resale, use, or disposal of the goods will accrue, either directly or indirectly, to the seller, or to any person associated in business with the seller:

2 persons are treated as being associated in business with each other if, whether directly or indirectly, either of them has any interest in the business or property of the other, or both have a common interest in any business or property, or some third person has an interest in the business or property of both of them:
in determining a fair market value in accordance with this clause, the chief executive must—
use only those prices that represent a sale between buyers and sellers independent of each other; and
exclude from the price any excise duty and goods and services tax.
The fair market value of any goods must be determined by applying sequentially subclauses (3) to (8) to the first subclause under which fair market value can be determined.

The fair market value of any goods on the date given by clause 12 is the lowest price for which identical goods in the same or substantially the same quantities are generally sold at that date in the open market in New Zealand on sales freely offered and made on ordinary trade terms by a licensee of a manufacturing area, other than a contractor.

If, in the chief executive’s opinion, the fair market value cannot be determined under subclause (3), it is the lowest price for which identical goods in quantities different from those being sold are generally sold at that date in the open market in New Zealand on sales freely offered and made on ordinary trade terms by a licensee of a manufacturing area, other than a contractor.

If, in the chief executive’s opinion, the fair market value cannot be determined under subclause (4), it is the lowest price for which similar goods in the same or substantially the same quantities are generally sold at that date in the open market in New Zealand on sales freely offered and made on ordinary trade terms by a licensee of a manufacturing area, other than a contractor.

If, in the chief executive’s opinion, the fair market value cannot be determined under subclause (5), it is the lowest price for which similar goods in quantities different from those being sold are generally sold at that date in the open market in New Zealand on sales freely offered and made on ordinary trade terms by a licensee of a manufacturing area, other than a contractor.

If, in the chief executive’s opinion, the fair market value cannot be determined under subclause (6), the chief executive must determine the price that the goods would generally fetch at the retail level and deduct from that price an amount that reasonably represents the profit margin and other costs beyond the manufacturing level on those goods.

If, in the chief executive’s opinion, the fair market value cannot be determined under subclause (7), the chief executive must determine the value of the goods by taking the costs of the production of the goods and adding an amount that reasonably represents the profit margin and other costs to the manufacturing level of those goods.

prov.heading: Excise-equivalent duty levied in respect of imported goods specified in Part B of Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table

para.text: Excise-equivalent duty is levied in respect of any goods specified in Part B of the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table that are imported.

prov.heading: Rate of excise-equivalent duty

para.text: Excise-equivalent duty levied in respect of any goods is levied at the applicable rate of duty specified in Part B of the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table.
If the rate is, or includes, an ad valorem rate, the value of the goods must be determined in accordance with Schedule 4 for the purpose of applying the ad valorem rate.

part.heading: Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table
part.crosshead: Setting out, and access to, Table

prov.heading: Order in Council to set out Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table

para.text: The Minister must recommend to the Governor-General that the Governor-General make an Order in Council, to come into force on the specified date, that sets out the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table, and the Governor-General may make the order.

The Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table set out in the order under subclause (1) must be the same as the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table under the Customs and Excise Act 1996 as that table will be in force immediately before the specified date but with the amendments set out in Part 5 of this schedule.

The rates of duty set out in the amendments in Part 5 of this schedule are the same as certain rates of duty set out in the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table under the Customs and Excise Act 1996 as at 1 July 2017.

Subclause (5) applies if any of those rates of duty set out in the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table under the Customs and Excise Act 1996 as at 1 July 2017 is changed on 1 or more occasions before the specified date by an Order in Council made under Part 7 of that Act.

The amendments in Part 5 of this schedule must be incorporated into the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table set out in the order under subclause (1) with the rates of duty changed as appropriate to reflect the changes to any rates of duty as referred to in subclause (4).
In this clause, specified date has the meaning given to that term in section 2(5).
In this Act,—
Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Order means the order made under subclause (1)
Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table means the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table set out in the order made under subclause (1).

prov.heading: Access to Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table

para.text: The chief executive must—
ensure that copies of the documents referred to in subclause (2) are available for purchase at a reasonable price at the places specified in a notice given under subsection (5).
The documents are as follows:
the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Order as originally made:
an order under clause 20, 21, 23, or 25(1):
a notice under clause 25(2):
the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table as in force from time to time.
Every version of the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table that is published or made available under subclause (1) in accordance with subclause (2)﻿(c) must—
indicate that it is that table as in force at the beginning of a stated date; and
list all the enactments that are made before the stated date and that amend, modify, or revoke and replace that table.

Nothing in this clause prevents the chief executive from ensuring that other information is published or made available with the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table, so long as in doing so the chief executive ensures that it is indicated clearly that the other information does not form part of, or have legal effect as part of, that table.
The chief executive must give notice in the Gazette specifying where copies of the documents referred to in subclause (2) can be purchased.

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 17(1)﻿(a): repealed, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).
Schedule 3 clause 17(1)﻿(b): amended, on 29 October 2019, by section 4 of the Legislation (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2019 (2019 No 59).
Schedule 3 clause 17(2)﻿(b): replaced, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).
Schedule 3 clause 17(2)﻿(ba): inserted, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).
Schedule 3 clause 17(5): inserted, on 29 October 2019, by section 4 of the Legislation (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2019 (2019 No 59).

prov.heading: Evidence of documents published or made available under clause 17

para.text: Every copy of the documents referred to in clause 17(2) purporting to be published or made available under the authority of the chief executive is, unless the contrary is shown, treated—
as being a correct copy of the document; and
as having been so published or made available.

prov.heading: Interpretation of certain terms used in Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table

para.text: The following provisions apply for the purposes of the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table:
alcoholic strength means alcoholic strength by volume at a temperature of 20ºC:

the alcoholic strength by volume of a mixture of water and ethyl alcohol is the ratio of the volume of alcohol present in the mixture at 20ºC to the total volume of the mixture at the same temperature:
the terms percent volume and % vol are used to express alcoholic strength in parts of alcohol per 100 parts of the mixture:
per litre, for all items under the heading “Fuels” in Part A or B of the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table, means the quantity of product expressed in litres at a temperature of 15°C.

part.crosshead: Powers to modify or amend Table

prov.heading: Power to suspend and modify rates of duty

para.text: The Governor-General—
may, by Order in Council, suspend the rates of duty specified in the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table in whole or in part; and
may, by the same or a subsequent Order in Council, provide for a rate of duty that is lower than a suspended rate of duty to apply instead of the suspended rate until the suspension ends.
An order under this clause must be confirmed by an Act (see subpart 3 of Part 5 of the Legislation Act 2019).

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 20(2): inserted, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).

prov.heading: Power to change rates of duty on alcoholic products and tobacco products

para.text: The Governor-General may, by Order in Council, amend the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table so as to change a rate of duty that applies to an alcoholic product or a tobacco product.
A change in a rate of duty that applies to an alcoholic product may come into force only on 1 July in any calendar year.
A change in a rate of duty that applies to a tobacco product may come into force only on 1 January in any calendar year.
A new rate of duty that results from a change made by an order under this clause must not exceed the rate given by the following formula:
where—
is the index number of the Consumers Price Index (less credit services subgroup) for—
the quarter ending on 31 March in the calendar year in which the new rate is to come into force, if the rate applies to an alcoholic product; or
the quarter ending on 30 September immediately before the calendar year in which the new rate is to come into force, if the rate applies to a tobacco product

is the index number of the Consumers Price Index (less credit services subgroup) for the quarter ending 12 months before, and expressed on the same base quarter as, the relevant quarter specified in item a of this formula
is the current rate.
An order under this clause must be confirmed by an Act (see subpart 3 of Part 5 of the Legislation Act 2019).
In this clause,—
alcoholic product means any goods specified in—
any of items 99.05 to 99.50 in Part A of the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table; or
any of items 21.05 to 22.08 and 33.02 in Part B of that table

Consumers Price Index (less credit services subgroup) means the Consumers Price Index (less credit services subgroup) published by Statistics New Zealand or, if that index ceases to be published, any measure certified by the Government Statistician as being equivalent to that index
tobacco product means any goods specified in—
item 99.60, 99.65, or 99.67 in Part A of the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table; or
item 24.02, 24.03, or 24.04 in Part B of that table.

Schedule 3 clause 21(4) formula item b: amended, on 1 September 2022, by section 107(1) of the Data and Statistics Act 2022 (2022 No 39).
Schedule 3 clause 21(5): repealed, on 1 January 2020 (but without affecting any new rates imposed before or on that date by any Orders in Council to which it applies), by Schedule 1 clause 33(1).
Schedule 3 clause 21(5A): inserted, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).

Schedule 3 clause 21(6) Consumers Price Index (less credit services subgroup): inserted, on 1 September 2022, by section 107(1) of the Data and Statistics Act 2022 (2022 No 39).
Schedule 3 clause 21(6) tobacco product paragraph (b): amended, on 25 May 2022, by section 6(2) of the Customs and Excise (Tobacco Products) Amendment Act 2022 (2022 No 28).

prov.heading: Rates of duties for tobacco products increased by 10% if not indexed on 1 January in 2018 to 2020

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 22: repealed, on 1 January 2020 (but without affecting any amendments it makes on or before that date), by Schedule 1 clause 33(2).

prov.heading: Power to change rates of duty on motor spirits

para.text: The Governor-General may, by Order in Council, amend the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table so as to change a rate of duty that applies to a motor spirit.
A change in a rate of duty that applies to a motor spirit may come into force only in the second or third financial year that follows the financial year in which the current rate came into force.
An order under this clause that increases a rate of duty must be confirmed by an Act (see subpart 3 of Part 5 of the Legislation Act 2019).
A determination under the definition of financial year in subclause (3) is secondary legislation (see Part 3 of the Legislation Act 2019 for publication requirements).
In this clause,—
financial year means a period of 12 months ending with 30 June or any other date determined for the purposes of this clause by the Minister of Finance
motor spirits means any fuels that are—
motor spirit, or fuels that contain motor spirit; or
specified in any of the following items:
items 99.75.05F, 99.75.13G, 99.75.23D, 99.75.29C, 99.75.33A, 99.75.37D, 99.75.51K, 99.75.59E, 99.75.73L, 99.75.81A, and 99.75.93E in Part A of the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table:

entry.emphasis: Legislation Act 2019 requirements for secondary legislation made under this clause

row.entry: The maker must notify it in the Gazette
It is not required to be presented to the House of Representative because a transitional exemption applies under Schedule 1 of the Legislation Act 2019
It may be disallowed by the House of Representatives

entry.emphasis: This note is not part of the Act.

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 23(2A): inserted, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).
Schedule 3 clause 23(2B): inserted, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).
Schedule 3 clause 23(3) financial year: amended, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).

prov.heading: Effect of disallowance or resolution to amend or replace Order in Council under clause 20, 21, or 23

para.text: This clause applies if—
an Order in Council is made under clause 20 or 21, or is made under clause 23 and increases a rate of duty; and
the House of Representatives resolves to—
disallow the order under section 116 of the Legislation Act 2019; or
amend or replace the order under section 119 of that Act.
Any duty collected under the order in excess of the duty otherwise payable must, so far as that resolution provides, be refunded.

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 24: replaced, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).

prov.heading: Powers to amend Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table for other limited purposes

para.text: The Governor-General may, by Order in Council, amend the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table by—

revoking, inserting, or amending any heading, heading number, subheading, item, or item number or the title of any Part, section, chapter, or subchapter of the Tariff referred to in the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table in any manner necessary to ensure that that table conforms to the Tariff; or

revoking, suspending, or amending a provision of the notes forming part of the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table, or by inserting a new provision in the notes, for the purpose of ensuring the proper operation of the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table; or
revoking, suspending, inserting, or amending a statistical unit in the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table.
The chief executive may also, by notice, revoke, suspend, insert, or amend a statistical unit in the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table.
No amendment made under this clause may alter the duties or exemptions from duty under this Act applicable to goods classified under an item or heading so amended.

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 25(2): amended, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).
Heading: replaced, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).

prov.heading: Application of Legislation Act 2019 to Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Order and related instruments

para.text: The following are secondary legislation (see Part 3 of the Legislation Act 2019 for publication requirements):
the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Order:
an order under clause 20, 21, 23, or 25(1):
a notice under clause 25(2).
However, in relation to that legislation,—
section 67(d)﻿(i) of the Legislation Act 2019 does not apply; and
section 26 of that Act is subject to clauses 21(2) and (3) and 23(2).

history.history-note: Schedule 3 clause 26: replaced, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).

part.heading: International organisations based in New Zealand, etc

prov.heading: Power to suspend, remit, refund, or create exemptions from duties for cases involving international organisations based in New Zealand, etc

para.text: The Governor-General may, by Order in Council, suspend, order the remission or refund of, or create exemptions from excise duty and excise-equivalent duty in respect of goods or classes of goods that are manufactured in New Zealand or imported and that—
are supplied solely for the use of organisations, expeditions, or other bodies that—
are approved by the chief executive; and

are established or temporarily based in New Zealand under an agreement or arrangement entered into by or on behalf of the Government of New Zealand with the Government of any other country or with the United Nations; or
are supplied solely for the use of persons temporarily resident in New Zealand for the purpose of serving as a member of an organisation, expedition, or other body to which paragraph (a) applies.
The chief executive may impose conditions in respect of goods or a class of goods to which an order made under this clause relates.
An order under subclause (1) is secondary legislation (see Part 3 of the Legislation Act 2019 for publication requirements).

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 3 columns that are grouped under the heading Legislation Act 2019 requirements for secondary legislation made under this clause.

row.entry: PCO must publish it on the legislation website and notify it in the Gazette

part.heading: Amendments to be made to Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table as in force under Customs and Excise Act 1996

prov.heading: Introduction

para.text: This Part sets out the amendments that must be made for the purposes of clause 16(2) to the Excise and Excise-equivalent Duties Table under the Customs and Excise Act 1996 as that table will be in force immediately before the specified date (as that term is defined in section 2(5)).

prov.heading: Amendments to notes

In note 5, replace imposed pursuant to section 75 of the Customs and Excise Act 1996 with levied under clause 14 of Schedule 3 of the Customs and Excise Act 2018.
